author: yaye.work
description: If you are frustrated with making tables in your notes, install this. Summon the interactive table via the ```Table``` block. Just drag to resize and reorder, hover to insert, click to edit. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: yaye.work
manifest.description: Summon an interactive table with the Table block. Just drag to resize and reorder, hover to insert, click to edit.
manifest.name: Better Tables

name: Better Tables

releases.author: yaye-work
releases.description:
## What's new in 0.2.1

**Spreadsheet-style cell selection** — click a cell to select it, click again (or press **Enter** / **F2**) to edit. Prefer the old behavior? Turn on **Settings → Better Tables → Quick text edit** to make a single click edit immediately.

**Cleaner selection outline** — the drag-selection frame is now a single overlay drawn above the grid (accent colour at 80%), so cell borders never cut through it. Selection fill is lighter, too.

**Align follows the selection** — right-click a multi-column drag selection and the alignment applies to every column it spans, not just the one under the cursor.

**Editing feels tighter** — clicking another cell while editing commits the current cell and selects the new one; a click after editing lands cleanly on the next cell.

Thanks for using Better Tables! Bug reports and feature requests are welcome in the issues — and if it's useful to you, you can [buy me a coffee ☕](https://buymeacoffee.com/yaye.work).

releases.author: yaye-work
releases.description:
## What's new in 0.2.0 **Column alignment** — right-click any cell to set the column's text alignment (left, center, right). Stored as standard Markdown separator-row syntax (`:---:`, `---:`), so it round-trips cleanly with any Markdown tool. While editing a cell, alignment items appear in Obsidian's native context menu. **Drag-select** — click and drag across cells to select a rectangular block. Then: - **Delete / Backspace** to clear the selection - **Cmd+C** to copy as tab-separated values (pastes into other tables or spreadsheets) - **Right-click** for Copy and Clear contents in the context menu - **Escape** or click outside to deselect **Keyboard navigation** — arrow keys step between cells (at the edge of a cell's text), Tab moves right (and creates a new row from the last cell), Enter moves down (and creates a new row from the last row). **Edge resize** — drag the lines between rows and columns to resize them precisely. **In-cell line breaks** — Shift+Enter or Cmd+Enter inserts a line break inside a cell. Stored as `
` in the Markdown source. **Delete-table button** — hover the block to reveal a trash icon next to Obsidian's edit-block control. One click removes the entire table.

releases.author: yaye-work
releases.description:
New: - Keyboard navigation: arrow keys move between cells, Tab/Enter on the last cell grow the table - Cmd+A selects all text within a cell - Resize the rightmost column and bottommost row (edge handles) - In-cell line breaks via Cmd+Enter or Shift+Enter (stored as
) - Delete-table button at the block's top-right, next to 'edit this block'

releases.author: yaye-work
releases.description: Restores the first Tab/Enter navigation fix that was accidentally dropped from the 0.1.2 build.

releases.author: yaye-work
releases.description: Fixes: first Tab/Enter now reliably moves between cells, text selection works in cells, layout chrome re-aligns when themes change.

releases.author: yaye-work
releases.description: Bug fixes: reliable first-click add row/column, fixed save race that corrupted blocks, fixed ghost flash on re-render.
